message:
Replace debug-string group keys with typed ScalarKey across fp-frame

Groupby, mode, describe, pivot, pivot_table, and categorical APIs have
been hashing and comparing Scalar values by first rendering them through
`format!("{val:?}")`. That worked for simple cases but produced a long
tail of subtle parity bugs against pandas:

- Debug formatting collapses distinct types that share a Display (e.g.
  `Int64(1)` vs `Bool(true)`) depending on downstream parsing, and
  depends on Rust-level Debug impls that were never intended as a
  stable key format.
- Strings like `"Utf8(\\"x\\")"` had to be rebuilt back into labels by
  hand-rolled `strip_prefix("Utf8(\\"")` unwrapping, once in pivot and
  once in pivot_table, which silently differed.
- Ordering on debug strings gave the wrong sort for numeric groupby
  keys (`"Int64(10)" < "Int64(2)"`), forcing ad-hoc workarounds.
- Missing-value handling leaked through `format!` for `NaN`, `NaT`,
  and `Null` inconsistently.

This commit introduces a structured hash/equality key and uses it
everywhere Scalar values previously round-tripped through strings:

- `ScalarKey` derives `Clone, Copy, Hash, PartialEq, Eq` so it can live
  inside HashMap/HashSet keys and composite Vec<ScalarKey> keys without
  cloning string buffers per row.
- `scalar_key_cmp` + `composite_key_cmp` define a total order that
  matches pandas' group sort: Bool < Int64 < Float64 (compared via
  total_cmp) < Utf8 < Null, with numeric cross-type comparisons
  promoting through f64, and a null rank (Null < NaN < NaT) for stable
  ordering of missing buckets.
- `Series::mode` uses ScalarKey to count by value identity instead of
  by Debug string, fixing a case where distinct scalars with identical
  Debug representations collapsed into one mode.
- `Series::categorical_from_values` / `CategoricalAccessor::set_categories`
  map old categories through ScalarKey so mixed-type category arrays
  no longer lose codes when debug formatting of a Scalar changes.
- `SeriesGroupBy::build_groups` now returns `(labels, order_keys,
  groups)` so downstream aggregations (sum/mean/std/count/first/last/
  size/agg) look up the HashMap by the actual key instead of a parallel
  `Vec<String>` rebuilt from labels.
- `DataFrame::describe` tracks `(Scalar, count)` per ScalarKey so the
  "top" frequency result reports the real scalar value instead of its
  Debug rendering.
- `DataFrame::pivot_table` and `DataFrame::pivot` use
  `HashMap<(ScalarKey, ScalarKey), _>` for the cell map, carry clean
  column labels alongside the keys, and drop both copies of the
  hand-rolled "strip Utf8(\\"...\\")" name cleaner (the label is now
  derived once at discovery time).
- `DataFrameGroupBy::build_groups` uses `Vec<ScalarKey>` composite keys
  and sorts via `composite_key_cmp`, so sorted groupby on numeric
  columns now produces numeric order rather than lexicographic order.
- `DataFrameGroupBy::get_group` matches by human label or by the
  original debug-string form (kept only as a compatibility path for
  callers that still pass the legacy key shape), no longer by the
  internal HashMap key.

This is a pure refactor of the hashing/ordering layer: no public API
signatures change, but several parity tests that depended on the
debug-string ordering now produce the correct pandas ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
